parsed_review.summary: CP00-351 is a clean, descriptor-only fixture slice (RP11.P05.M05 "Permission And Audit Binding", units RP11.P05.M05.S08-S17, risk class A, 10 units) that I independently verified through Read/Grep/Glob only. Structural consistency holds end-to-end: the pack binding (packages/time-expense/src/registry.js:163-178) matches the review baseline, the plan pack (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json:413), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and the contract current_pack/fixture_slice_descriptor. The deliverable distribution {implementation:4, security_audit:2, fixture:1, test:2, hermes_evidence:1}=10 and the single micro-title "Permission And Audit Binding" x10 are confirmed against included_units. All 7 required public exports exist (service.js:1385,1418; validators.js:2006,2041,2124,2145,2168) and are surfaced via index.js export *. I traced the row-extras inheritance chain and confirmed the four new rows (fixture_manifest, golden_test, failure_test, hermes_fixture_evidence) draw their guard fields from CP350_ROW_EXTRAS (service.js:1246-1249), matching the contract projection (contracts/time-expense-core-contract.json:12219-12313) exactly; the validator enforces deepEqual between contract and code-generated descriptor (scripts/validate-rp11-time-expense-core-contract.mjs:501-505) and exercises coverage/slice/Hermes/Claude/handoff against the real contract and plan. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in freezeCp351Result (service.js:1327-1377) and the slice validator's per-row security guards (denied/cross-tenant/audit-hint/security-trimming/AI/test/Hermes). Authority boundary is correct: Claude is not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false), enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, human approval is required, and runtime opening is deferred to CP00-352; Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor-level evidence labeling, not product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). There is no production_ready overclaim: the manifest reports production_ready:false and status implemented_pending_review, with the Hermes packet flagged only as production_ready_candidate. Review-evidence hardening checks pass: the auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON, non-zero, not fenced/tool-shaped/malformed), the prompt is within the size limit, only read-only tools are configured, and the post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this pre-review stage and gated by the remaining hardened sequence. Unrelated dirty/untracked files are correctly excluded from pack scope. No P0-P3 defects were found; final human approval remains the authority for the enterprise trust claim and runtime opening.
